key_insight: Phosphorus-doped triangulene can be assembled into superatomic graphene with tunable flat bands and intrinsic magnetic behavior.
engineering_relevance: This bottom-up strategy offers a programmable route to designer flat-band materials for spintronic and correlated-electron device concepts.
method: On-surface bottom-up synthesis of superatomic graphene from phosphorus-doped triangulene precursors.
Scanning tunneling microscopy/spectroscopy to resolve lattice structure and electronic bands.
Density functional theory to identify orbital origin of the flat bands and predict magnetism; oxygen functionalization used for tuning.
evidence_signal: STM/STS directly observed the honeycomb lattice and characteristic Dirac and flat-band electronic signatures, consistent with DFT predictions of p_x,y-derived flat bands and half-metallicity.
watch_item: The platform is demonstrated at the surface-assembled molecular scale, so translating the tunable magnetic/flat-band behavior into robust device architectures may require careful control of defects, ordering, and functionalization.
paper_title: Tunable Flat Bands and magnetism in Triangulene-based Superatomic Graphene
